item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The man

The myth

The legend

God bless you D-Class

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice. I liked reading it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dafriq?

item.title: When "strong religious belief' is stated...
item.content:encoded:
Does that mean ANY faith?

Let's say I believe that Dragons are divine beings and gods, and have my own made up prayers and beliefs…would that work?

Do I need to be Christian? Abrahamic? Can it be a faith only you believe in?

If the article explained, I am most likely just not seeing it either because I'm to stupid or just blind.

Edit: Nevermind, I was just blind and didn't see the part explaining it. My apologies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. These beings that I compare to demons are really cool, especially with the way the reproduce. It makes perfect sense that prayer would be the only way to kill pure evil. I wish I could give more than one +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What do you mean they predicted 11/9?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good writing for sure! Most of all, I really liked the way this ends, with an apparent worse-than-a-nobody D-class walking into hell… and then, like so often happens in real life, this apparent nobody turns out to be made of much sterner stuff than it seems. One of the easiest +1s I've given in a while, well done!

item.title: A Little Heart-to-Heart
item.content:encoded:
“SCP-058 was first encountered at Site ██ as it came out of [DATA EXPUNGED].”

“SCP-058 was first encountered at Site 54 as it came out of SCP-1983-1.”

I guess that pile of hearts was just a larval stage.

item.title: Re: Necromancy.
item.content:encoded:
That was my question too, but after giving it some thought, I think the answer may be that because the inside is an anomalously larger space, the door opens inward to a degree making it effectively impossible to close from outside, regardless of how you try to do it. But what do I know? :)

(Also, how would you close an inward-opening door through its hinges?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: so they predicted the September 11?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For the same reason they sent people in there in the first place? The Foundation routinely throws people at dangerous weird stuff to see if it is still weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the few skips that actually spooked me when I got into SCP. Love this one to death and back.

Small issue though, even if the anomaly disappeared, why would the Foundation take the risk and enter the house anyways? Who's saying the skip wasn't just in a dormant mode or something?

item.title: Necromancy.
item.content:encoded: What if the Foundation had used some sort of scooper or device to reach inside 1983 and close the door? Would the device just be dragged in? Couldn't the door be closed through the hinges? Is it like a black hole where you get sucked in?

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

I've got a pretty simple one for you here. The tale Mr. Parker from the D-Class contest features a little background and character building for D-14134, and one of the mentions of the character can easily in-line link there. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-1983 is presumed to have been neutralized by D-14134, who was posthumously awarded the Foundation Star (one of only two awarded to Class-D Personnel). Due to information contained in Document 1983-15, it is believed that the anomaly was not localized, as previously believed, and renewed resources have gone into attempting to locate similar incidents.

To this:

SCP-1983 is presumed to have been neutralized by D-14134, who was posthumously awarded the Foundation Star (one of only two awarded to Class-D Personnel). Due to information contained in Document 1983-15, it is believed that the anomaly was not localized, as previously believed, and renewed resources have gone into attempting to locate similar incidents.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Absolutely wonderful. A pretty strong scp concept on it's own, but the well written journal format really elevates it. Wonderful tone. Goes to show how good execution goes a long way. Pouring one out for that bad ass nameless d class. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everyone's talking about Kingdom Hearts and I'm just over here like, "Dudes. Smash Bros. The Subspace Emissary." I wonder if TVTropes has a trope name for worlds cut up and seen back together like this.

IIRC I +1'd this ages ago, but I'll go double-check.

item.title: Re: Inspiration
item.content:encoded: Understood, I'll write one now.

item.title: Re: Inspiration
item.content:encoded: PM would be best.

item.title: Re: Inspiration
item.content:encoded: No, sorry I was just trying to create a unique typing style, and in that way it might stick in peoples minds a bit better.

item.title: Re: Inspiration
item.content:encoded:
Sir B, are you using bolded text to try to make it stand out from the rest of the discussion?

If so, there's no need, especially since there's no guarantee that someone will routinely check certain pages for new comments. Just send the user in question a wikidot PM.

item.title: Re: Inspiration
item.content:encoded: How would you like me to contact you in this aspect?

item.title: Re: Inspiration
item.content:encoded: I have absolutely no problem with this so long as you follow the licensing guide. I'd be delighted, in fact. I only ask that you keep me updated on it.

item.title: Inspiration
item.content:encoded:
DrEverettMann, do you mind if I use ideas from your scp to create a game, I was going to name it "Fear the Light" and have creatures like the ones described. I was mainly going to use the 'they can only harm you in light' idea to bring up the tension a bit.

If you'd like more information, I could hit you up with my email and we could talk.

B

P.S. Sorry if this is the wrong place to ask for this.

item.title: Admin Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Noting that a recent edit by StevenThePotato was reverted, as it changed the wordin of a phrase that altered the meaning of a sentence, which is considered a stylistic edit and as such requires permission from the original author or staff to be made.

Editors, if you're not fixing (unintentionally mistaken) grammar, mechanics, or formatting, please obtain permission prior to making changes to a page. This is noted in the Site Rules.

item.title: Re: +666
item.content:encoded:
Ha, I came to the comments to mention the 666 rating.

NOBODY TOUCH IT! ;)
This is cheesy as hell, but I happened upon this one again right as the post counter hit +666, which was rather amusing given the content. Congrats, and thanks for the accidental chuckle. ;)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How come someone hasn't turned this into a video game yet? It seems rife with possibility. Random locations, shape changing enemies, etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone has made a roblox game about it, it's surprisingly well made, maybe you should check it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes please!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want this made into a game all it's own. Has anybody done that yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted for badass D-class. I'm pleasantly suprised that Foundation awarded him with something more than "huh, they can actually do something useful?". I would like to know what the other badass D-class did.

item.title: That Foundation worker though
item.content:encoded:
"They find some sort of pitch-black things floating around. Some brain at the Foundation has seen something like them before, figures out how to kill them. Silver bullets, and pray to God as you fire."

[EARLIER, AT SITE 19…]

"…Name?"

"Harrison."

"… And your credentials here include…?"

"Killing Shadow-Daemons with silver bullets and the prayers of the damnned, yes, that's correct."

"… Sounds reasonable. You're hired."

+1, for creating that scenario in my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it means "I can't pray anymore. I mean, I can say the words but there's no more belief behind them".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What does "not and mean it" mean?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was reading the note and was all like :O till I noticed. Literally got my mouyh open for a while. +1 from the darkness in my heart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uber nerd mode: yeah, they resemble the heartless in the first game, but those damned things weren't some self procreating phenomenon, they were, to avoid rambling spoilers, created by someone human. In short, KH is hella more complex than shadow creatures stealing hearts to make more shadow creatures.

Also, upboated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man you all play too many video games I didn't even think about Kingdom Hearts until someone mentioned it in the comments lmao. I mean I know that the 'darkness of the heart' schtick has been done before but this still feels fresh as a field of summer daisies which contrasts wonderfully with the image of a mound of hearts might I add— good keter best keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP reminds me of the Heartless from Kingdom Hearts. That's what I think these are and I'm sticking to it. Take my up-vote, take all my damn up-votes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe…
OR
It's not said anywhere that he DID destroyed those hearts. Shadows stopped coming, but no one know for sure what happened.
I kinda feel it's same story as 354. "It stopped for good, or is it just resting/charging up?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of like the idea that he just stomped on them, partly because it's an interesting mental image and partly because sometimes the mundane solution is the one that works. That being said, author input is always interesting to hear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Perfect story. Intriguing procedures, detailed description, yet leaving some to the imagination, idea creepy as hell and note/exploration log revealing (partially, just fine) the mystery. Perfect.
I'm just wondering, how did D-14134 destroyed those hearts? He burned them, or what? That's the only thing that bugs me.
Anyway, grat job. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No. No I did not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"but I can't pray anymore, not and mean it."

do you mean "but I can't pray anymore and mean it."?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We don't have a rule forbidding people from commenting on old articles. If you're thinking about "necroposting," that refers to replying to a comment made a long time ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't care if I'm commenting on an old article, but this will always be my favorite SCP ever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This reminded me very strongly of a lot of the elements in John Dies in the End and that universe. I love that book, and I love this article, so +1. This is good stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Who knows what evil lurks in the hearts of men? The Shadow knows.
Good work, eh? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is crazy good. I could feel my heartbeat for the entire last half of the note. +1 and placement in my top ten skips ever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this, it's horror, detailed yet not too tale-like, and it just sorta ticks all my sweet spots

+1 with the fury of a thousand beating hearts

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dare I say? Better ketter than 682! There its done not going back!

All around perfection for me, I see a new scp video game!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If that was the case i'd still upvote for the fact that they would be goth-heartless instead of the clowns kingdom hearts has created. Yes, they have a clown-heartless.

Addendum: Audio readings have confirmed that when human subjects vacate the area around SCP-1983, a voice can be heard inside SCP-1983 which begs the subject to stay. However, in 100% of recorded instances, said subjects appeared to have not heard the voice.

also +300 woo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AFAICT it uses a bunch of tropes that are extremely common in usage, but also appear all together in Kingdom Hearts.

It doesn't appear to be even accidental plagiarism from what I've investigated, although it's good that the question was raised.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …It's similar to KH, but not in any way that suggests plagiarism to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That you assholes play too many video games.

item.title: Re: nvm, Kiwi IRC gets around the filter
item.content:encoded: Apparently the Heartless from Kingdom Hearts are similar to this. That's what Google tells me, anyways, I've never played the game(s).

item.title: nvm, Kiwi IRC gets around the filter
item.content:encoded: I'm going to need this explained to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oooooh.
Mann, what do you have to say for yourself?
